medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.verbatim_instruction: Take 500 milligrams by mouth every 6 hours as needed for pain. Do not take more than 3000 milligrams in 24 hours.

medications.name: Oxycodone
medications.verbatim_instruction: Take 5 milligrams by mouth every 6 hours as needed for severe pain. Do not drive while taking this.

medications.name: Enoxaparin
medications.verbatim_instruction: Inject 40 milligrams under the skin once daily to prevent blood clots for 14 days after surgery.

prescribed.wound_care: Keep the dressing clean and dry. Change the outer gauze once a day. Do not soak the knee in a bath until your follow-up visit.
prescribed.activity: Use your walker for all walking. Do your knee-bending exercises three times a day. Ice for 20 minutes after exercises.

red_flag_thresholds.wound: spreading redness beyond 2 cm, pus/foul drainage, or wound opening

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.verbatim_instruction: Take 650 milligrams by mouth every 8 hours for pain. Do not exceed 3000 milligrams in 24 hours.

medications.name: Aspirin
medications.verbatim_instruction: Take 81 milligrams by mouth twice daily to prevent blood clots for 30 days.

prescribed.wound_care: Leave the surgical glue and strips in place. Keep the area dry for 48 hours. It is normal to see a small amount of clear or pink fluid on day 1 and 2. Follow your hip precautions: do not bend the hip past 90 degrees.
prescribed.activity: Bear weight as tolerated with the walker. Do not cross your legs. Elevate the leg when resting. Ankle pumps every hour while awake.

red_flag_thresholds.wound: spreading redness, pus, or increasing warmth

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.verbatim_instruction: Take 500 milligrams by mouth every 6 hours as needed for pain. Do not take more than 3000 milligrams in 24 hours.

medications.name: Enoxaparin
medications.verbatim_instruction: Inject 40 milligrams under the skin once daily to prevent blood clots for 14 days after surgery.

prescribed.wound_care: Incision may be closed with staples. Keep it clean and dry. Some mild itching as it heals is normal. Staples come out at your follow-up.
prescribed.activity: Advance to a cane as balance allows. Continue physical therapy exercises. Short walks are encouraged.

red_flag_thresholds.wound: spreading redness, pus/foul drainage, or wound opening
